name: IT
evidencePatterns: application, website, network, cybersecurity, database, automation, or support workflow
requirements, design notes, implementation artifacts, testing evidence, debugging logs, and user feedback
riskQuestions: Does the project demonstrate a specific technical skill beyond basic tool use?
Is there testing evidence from real or realistic users?
Are privacy, access, or security concerns documented?

name: Culinary
evidencePatterns: menu, recipe development, service plan, food production, costing, safety, or hospitality event component
test batches, costing sheets, sanitation plans, feedback, revisions, and final product documentation
riskQuestions: Are food safety and sanitation expectations explicit?
Does the student document testing and revision rather than only a final product?
Are cost, sourcing, and timing realistic?

name: Hospitality & Marketing
evidencePatterns: event plan, campaign, customer experience improvement, brand package, market research, or service design
audience research, campaign assets, budget, timeline, feedback, metrics, and revision notes
riskQuestions: Is the target audience clear?
Are marketing or hospitality decisions tied to evidence?
Can the student show measurable impact or realistic evaluation?

name: Mechanical Technology
evidencePatterns: prototype, repair, fabrication, mechanical system, technical drawing, measurement, or diagnostic process
design sketches, tool and safety documentation, build logs, measurements, tests, failures, and revisions
riskQuestions: Are tool safety and material constraints documented?
Does the project show mechanical reasoning, precision, or diagnostics?
Is testing evidence strong enough to prove the outcome works?

name: Construction
evidencePatterns: build plan, model, structure, construction detail, materials plan, safety procedure, or client-style deliverable
measurements, drawings, materials list, cost estimate, safety checks, progress photos, and quality review
riskQuestions: Are safety, tools, and permissions clear before work begins?
Does the evidence show process and quality, not just completion?
Are measurements, materials, and constraints realistic?

name: Sports Medicine
evidencePatterns: training resource, injury prevention plan, wellness education, anatomy/physiology demonstration, or support protocol
research sources, ethical boundaries, accuracy checks, audience feedback, and practical demonstration evidence
riskQuestions: Is the project educational rather than unauthorized medical advice?
Are sources accurate and appropriate?
Does the student document audience need and feedback?

name: Teaching & Training
evidencePatterns: lesson plan, instructional resource, tutoring sequence, classroom support tool, assessment, or training material
learning objective, audience analysis, lesson artifacts, feedback, reflection, and revision evidence
riskQuestions: Is there a clear learner and learning goal?
Does the project include feedback from learners or educators?
Are privacy and age-appropriate boundaries respected?

name: Early Childhood Education
evidencePatterns: developmentally appropriate activity, classroom material, family resource, observation tool, or early learning support
developmental rationale, safety notes, activity plan, adult feedback, child-appropriate reflection, and revision evidence
riskQuestions: Is the activity developmentally appropriate?
Are safety, supervision, and privacy boundaries clear?
Does the evidence show learning purpose rather than decoration?

name: Medical Professions
evidencePatterns: health education resource, clinical skills simulation, patient communication tool, safety protocol, or healthcare pathway project
research sources, scope boundaries, skill demonstration, feedback, revision, and reflection on professional ethics
riskQuestions: Is the project safely inside student scope and not providing diagnosis or treatment?
Are medical facts sourced and reviewed appropriately?
Does the evidence show professionalism, accuracy, safety, and ethical boundaries?
